name: Water
description: Pure water. Universal solvent.
easyDescription: Just water. Safe and clear.
moderateDescription: H₂O molecules held by hydrogen bonds. Neutral pH of 7.
complexDescription: Polar covalent molecule. Autoionization constant Kw = 1×10⁻¹⁴. Amphoteric — acts as both acid and base.

name: Hydrochloric Acid
description: Strong acid. Highly corrosive.
easyDescription: A very strong acid. Burns things it touches.
moderateDescription: Strong acid that fully dissociates in water, releasing H⁺ ions. Very corrosive to metals and skin.
complexDescription: Strong monoprotic acid. Complete dissociation: HCl → H⁺ + Cl⁻. Concentrated form fumes in air. Reacts with most metals, bases, and carbonates.

name: Sodium Hydroxide
description: Strong base. Caustic soda. Highly corrosive.
easyDescription: A powerful base. Just as dangerous as strong acid.
moderateDescription: Strong base that releases OH⁻ ions. Reacts violently with acids and generates heat.
complexDescription: Strong base, fully dissociates: NaOH → Na⁺ + OH⁻. Saponification agent. Highly exothermic neutralization with acids. Attacks amphoteric metals like aluminum.

name: Vinegar
description: Weak acid. Acetic acid solution.
easyDescription: Sour liquid from vinegar. Mild acid.
moderateDescription: Weak acid — only partially releases H⁺ ions. Safe to handle but reacts with bases and carbonates.
complexDescription: Weak monoprotic acid. Ka = 1.8×10⁻⁵. Partial dissociation equilibrium. Reacts with carbonates producing CO₂. Esterification with alcohols.

name: Baking Soda
description: Mild base. Sodium bicarbonate. Common household chemical.
easyDescription: White powder used in baking. Very safe mild base.
moderateDescription: Weak base. Reacts with acids to produce CO₂ gas. Familiar reaction with vinegar.
complexDescription: Amphoteric salt. Reacts with acids: NaHCO₃ + HCl → NaCl + H₂O + CO₂↑. Thermal decomposition above 50°C: 2NaHCO₃ → Na₂CO₃ + H₂O + CO₂.

name: Hydrogen Peroxide
description: Strong oxidizer. Bleaching agent.
easyDescription: Clear liquid that releases oxygen bubbles when it reacts.
moderateDescription: Oxidizing agent that decomposes to release O₂ gas. Reaction speeds up with catalysts like MnO₂.
complexDescription: Unstable oxidizer. Decomposition: 2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂↑. Catalytic decomposition with MnO₂ is highly exothermic. Used in elephant toothpaste reaction.

name: Ethanol
description: Flammable alcohol. Highly volatile.
easyDescription: Alcohol liquid. Catches fire easily. Handle carefully.
moderateDescription: Volatile flammable liquid. Evaporates quickly. Ignites below room temperature.
complexDescription: Primary alcohol. Flash point 13°C. Combustion: C₂H₅OH + 3O₂ → 2CO₂ + 3H₂O. Miscible with water. Oxidized to acetaldehyde then acetic acid.

name: Copper Sulfate
description: Vivid blue copper salt. Used as indicator.
easyDescription: Bright blue crystals or blue liquid. Beautiful color.
moderateDescription: Blue copper salt. Dissolves in water to make blue solution. Reacts with iron by displacement.
complexDescription: Transition metal salt. Cu²⁺ gives characteristic blue color. Displacement: Fe + CuSO₄ → FeSO₄ + Cu. Dehydration above 110°C turns white.

name: Sodium Chloride
description: Table salt. Neutral ionic compound.
easyDescription: Plain table salt. Very safe.
moderateDescription: Ionic compound. Dissolves in water. Raises boiling point of water slightly.
complexDescription: Ionic lattice structure. Boiling point elevation: ΔTb = iKbm. Electrolysis produces Na and Cl₂. Flame test: yellow-orange sodium emission.

name: Litmus Solution
description: pH indicator. Turns red in acid, blue in base.
easyDescription: Purple liquid that changes color. Red with acid, blue with base.
moderateDescription: pH indicator dye. Color change shows if a substance is acid or base.
complexDescription: Natural indicator. Red form (azolitmin) below pH 4.5. Blue form above pH 8.3. Intermediate purple between 4.5-8.3.

name: Phenolphthalein
description: pH indicator. Clear in acid, vivid pink in base.
easyDescription: Clear liquid that turns bright pink when base is added.
moderateDescription: Titration indicator. Colorless below pH 8.2, vivid magenta above pH 10.
complexDescription: Weak acid indicator. pKa ≈ 9.4. Lactone ring opens in basic conditions producing pink quinone form. Used in acid-base titrations.

name: Sulfuric Acid
description: Highly corrosive strong acid. Extremely dangerous.
easyDescription: Very dangerous acid. The most corrosive liquid in the lab.
moderateDescription: Extremely strong acid. Highly exothermic when mixed with water. Destroys organic material.
complexDescription: Diprotic strong acid. Dehydrating agent. Mixing with water: highly exothermic — always add acid to water, never water to acid. Reacts with most metals.

name: Ammonia Solution
description: Pungent weak base. Highly volatile.
easyDescription: Smelly liquid. A base that evaporates quickly.
moderateDescription: Volatile weak base. Strong smell. Reacts with acids to form ammonium salts.
complexDescription: Weak base. NH₃ + H₂O ⇌ NH₄⁺ + OH⁻. Kb = 1.8×10⁻⁵. Forms dense white smoke with HCl: NH₃ + HCl → NH₄Cl.

name: Iron Filings
description: Fine iron powder. Reactive metal.
easyDescription: Grey metal powder. Reacts with acids and rusts in air.
moderateDescription: Reactive metal. Dissolves in acid producing hydrogen gas. Displaces less reactive metals.
complexDescription: Transition metal. Reacts with HCl: Fe + 2HCl → FeCl₂ + H₂↑. Displacement: Fe + CuSO₄ → FeSO₄ + Cu. Oxidation states: Fe²⁺ and Fe³⁺.

name: Calcium Carbonate
description: Chalk/limestone. Reacts with acids.
easyDescription: White powder like chalk. Fizzes with acid.
moderateDescription: Carbonate salt. Reacts with all acids to produce CO₂ bubbles.
complexDescription: Carbonate. CaCO₃ + 2HCl → CaCl₂ + H₂O + CO₂↑. Thermal decomposition above 840°C: CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂. Basis of limestone chemistry.

name: Manganese Dioxide
description: Black catalyst. Dramatically speeds up H₂O₂ decomposition.
easyDescription: Black powder that makes hydrogen peroxide explode with foam.
moderateDescription: Catalyst for H₂O₂ decomposition. Causes rapid O₂ release. Not consumed in reaction.
complexDescription: Heterogeneous catalyst. Lowers activation energy of H₂O₂ → H₂O + ½O₂. Classic elephant toothpaste reaction. MnO₂ regenerated after reaction.

name: Universal Indicator
description: Shows full pH range as color spectrum.
easyDescription: Magic liquid that shows every pH as a different color.
moderateDescription: Mixture of indicators. Red at pH 1, orange at 3, yellow at 5, green at 7, blue at 9, purple at 11.
complexDescription: Multi-indicator solution. Color transitions across full pH range 1-14. Used for rough pH determination. More accurate than litmus, less precise than pH meter.

name: Sodium Thiosulfate
description: Reduces iodine. Used in clock reaction.
easyDescription: White crystals used in a famous chemistry clock experiment.
moderateDescription: Reducing agent. Reacts with acid to produce sulfur — solution turns milky white. Basis of the iodine clock reaction.
complexDescription: Thiosulfate ion S₂O₃²⁻. Reaction with HCl: Na₂S₂O₃ + 2HCl → 2NaCl + S↓ + SO₂ + H₂O. Rate depends on temperature — classic kinetics demonstration.

name: Potassium Permanganate
description: Deep purple oxidizer. Strong staining agent.
easyDescription: Deep purple crystals that turn water vivid purple instantly.
moderateDescription: Strong oxidizer. Brilliant purple color. Decolorizes when it oxidizes organic compounds.
complexDescription: Strong oxidizing agent. MnO₄⁻ reduced to Mn²⁺ (colorless) in acid, MnO₂ in neutral, Mn²⁺ in strong acid. Purple to colorless color change is visually dramatic.

name: Iodine Solution
description: Brown-black halogen solution. Turns starch dark blue.
easyDescription: Dark brown liquid. Turns black when it touches starch like bread or potato.
moderateDescription: Halogen in solution. Forms deep blue-black complex with starch molecules. Oxidizing agent.
complexDescription: I₂ in KI solution (Lugol's). Starch-iodine complex formed by I₃⁻ entering amylose helix. Oxidizing agent reduced to I⁻. Classic starch test.
